response.justification: Aisha Okonkwo demonstrates strong full-stack development skills and a diverse technical background, but her experience with C++ and industrial automation is limited. While she has worked with low-latency systems and multi-threading, her recent roles emphasize TypeScript, Node.js, and web technologies, which do not fully align with the core requirements of this role. However, her problem-solving abilities, mentorship experience, and exposure to event-driven architectures could be valuable.
strengths: Extensive experience with event-driven architectures and distributed systems (e.g., GISCollective, data integration at Sociomantic).
Proven leadership and mentorship skills through workshops, talks, and senior roles.
Strong problem-solving abilities and exposure to secure development practices (e.g., TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development).
concerns: Limited recent hands-on experience with C++ (last notable C++ work was in 2014–2015 at National Instruments).
Primary expertise lies in web and full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, React), not industrial automation or embedded systems.
No explicit mention of industrial cybersecurity standards (e.g., IEC 62443) or secure development lifecycle (SDL) practices.
